romanized: Ào Lì Ào Jiā Xīn Bǐng

region_of_origin: United States (Nabisco / Mondelez International, 1912); Singapore formulation by Mondelez Singapore
food_subgroup: Chocolate-flavoured sandwich cookie with vanilla cream filling; packaged biscuit / ultra-processed snack

ontology.foodon_label: sandwich cookie (closest parent — chocolate sandwich cookie with cream filling)
ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for branded Oreo biscuits. FOODON_03301710 (sandwich cookie) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Biscuits and cookies — sweet biscuits (closest parent; FoodEx2 does not have a branded Oreo entry)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A03SJ covers sweet biscuits broadly. No specific code exists for chocolate sandwich cookies with cream filling under the Oreo brand.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for Oreo-style sandwich cookies. Not clinically coded as a distinct food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 3 Oreo cookies, weighing approximately 34 g, as stated on the Mondelez Singapore Nutrition Information Panel.

nutrient_source.title: Oreo Original — Mondelez Singapore Nutrition Information Panel (NIP); cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central — Cookies, chocolate sandwich, with creme filling (FDC ID 18159)
nutrient_source.organization: Mondelez International / Mondelez Singapore (primary); USDA Agricultural Research Service (cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary values taken directly from the Singapore product NIP (Mondelez Singapore), which is an approved branded-food source. Values cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 18159 (Cookies, chocolate sandwich, with creme filling) for plausibility. Minor micronutrient values (selenium, magnesium, phosphorus, zinc, B-vitamins) supplemented from USDA SR Legacy / FDC where the Singapore NIP does not report them, documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of chocolate sandwich cookies (Oreo-style) — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (Glycemic Index Research Service, Sydney)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~65 (medium) sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for Oreo-style chocolate sandwich cookies with cream filling. Glycemic load calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 25.2g) / 100 ≈ 16.4 per 34g serving. Available carbohydrate per serving = total carbohydrate (74.4 × 0.34) − dietary fibre (2.6 × 0.34) = 25.3 − 0.88 ≈ 25.2g. Confidence 0.9 as the database entry covers the cookie category rather than the specific Singapore SKU.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium (100 mg per 34g serving) combined with very high sugar content (14g per serving); excess sugar intake is independently associated with elevated blood pressure via insulin-mediated sodium retention.
hypertension.guidance: Limit to 3 cookies (34g) per occasion; the sodium load per serving is moderate, but the very high sugar content (41.2g/100g) makes frequent consumption inadvisable for hypertensive patients managing weight and metabolic risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (41.2g/100g; 14g per 3-cookie serving) and medium GI (~65) producing a glycemic load of ~16 per serving — a clinically significant postprandial glucose challenge.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Not recommended as a regular snack for people with Type 2 Diabetes; if consumed, limit strictly to 3 cookies (34g) and pair with a protein or fat source to blunt the glycemic response; the 471 kcal/100g energy density also limits portion flexibility for weight-conscious T2DM patients.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (5.9g/100g; ~2g per serving) and very high sugar (41.2g/100g) are the primary cardiovascular concerns; trans fat is confirmed 0g following the 2015 Singapore reformulation to non-hydrogenated palm oil.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Occasional consumption of 3 cookies (34g) is unlikely to be acutely harmful for most CVD patients, but the high sugar and saturated fat content make regular consumption inadvisable; cholesterol is 0mg per serving, which is a minor positive.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high sugar content promotes insulin spikes with pro-inflammatory downstream effects; Dutch-processed cocoa provides minimal flavanol anti-inflammatory benefit at typical serving sizes.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Limit consumption due to the high sugar load; the anti-inflammatory benefit from cocoa flavanols in Oreo wafers is negligible at a 34g serving and does not offset the pro-inflammatory effect of 14g sugar per serving.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Oreo Original Sandwich Cookie is the world's bestselling cookie brand, manufactured by Mondelez International and sold widely across Singapore in supermarkets, convenience stores, school canteens, and online retailers. Each cookie consists of two circular Dutch-processed cocoa wafers sandwiching a sweet vanilla cream filling made primarily from sugar and palm oil. The Singapore formulation (Mondelez Singapore NIP) was reformulated in 2015 to replace hydrogenated fats with non-hydrogenated palm oil, eliminating trans fats (confirmed 0g). The standard serving is 3 cookies (~34g), providing approximately 160 kcal, 14g sugar, 6g fat, and 100mg sodium. Per 100g, Oreos are energy-dense (471 kcal), very high in sugar (41.2g), and moderate in fat (17.6g), with a medium glycemic index of ~65 and a glycemic load of ~16 per serving — a clinically significant postprandial glucose challenge. The primary clinical concern is the very high sugar content from the cream filling, making Oreos among the highest-sugar biscuits per gram available in Singapore. In the local food culture, Oreos are used as a bubble tea topping (Oreo ice blended), cheesecake base, frozen yoghurt topping, and baking ingredient, in addition to being consumed as a standalone snack, often with milk. The product is certified halal, vegan, and dairy-free. Allergens include wheat (gluten) and soy lecithin.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient values sourced directly from the Mondelez Singapore product NIP (an approved branded-food source), cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 18159. GI sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for the Oreo-style cookie category. All core macros and secondary nutrients are complete and plausible. Confidence is high for macros and Singapore-specific label values.
notes: 1. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per Mondelez Singapore NIP following the 2015 reformulation to non-hydrogenated palm oil. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per Singapore NIP — Oreo contains no animal-derived fat in the Singapore formulation. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_b12_mcg are 0.0: confirmed negligible/zero per Singapore NIP and USDA FDC 18159 for this product category. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1.0: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported on the Singapore NIP and not available in USDA FDC 18159 at the required precision for this specific SKU. water_g is set to -1.0: moisture content not reported on the Singapore NIP; USDA FDC 18159 reports approximately 2.5g/100g but this value is not confirmed for the Singapore formulation and is therefore excluded to avoid misrepresentation. 3. Micronutrient values for potassium, calcium, iron, magnesium, phosphorus, zinc, selenium, and B-vitamins are sourced from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 18159 (Cookies, chocolate sandwich, with creme filling) as the Singapore NIP does not report these fields. These values are plausible cross-references and are flagged as USDA-supplemented. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for the Oreo-style chocolate sandwich cookie category; confidence 0.9. GL per 34g serving calculated as (65 × 25.2) / 100 ≈ 16.4. 5. FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 is the closest parent class (sandwich cookie); no exact branded entry exists. FoodEx2 A03SJ (sweet biscuits) is the closest parent code. 6. Hindi alias 'ओरियो सैंडविच कुकी' is a transliteration; no established Hindi-language common name exists for this product in Singapore. 7. Q8 self-check performed (iteration 3): all string values in aliases_by_language verified present verbatim in flat aliases array — english: 7 entries all present; chinese: 4 entries all present; malay: 1 entry present; tamil: 2 entries both present; hindi: 1 entry present; romanized: 2 entries both present. Total flat aliases: 17 entries. No discrepancies found.
